metadata.notes: Dates are labeled Week 1-13. Map to actual calendar dates when seeding. Mix targets: 40% engagement (BvM, Confessional), 30% awareness (Stats, Features), 20% shares (Quiz), 10% UGC (Confessional CTA).

content.caption:
Welcome to The Confessional.

Anonymous. Unfiltered. Judgment-free.

Swipe to read real confessions from real brides planning real (chaotic) weddings.

Got one of your own? Link in bio.

#DesiWedding #WeddingPlanning #TheMarigold #BrideConfessions #WeddingChaos
content.ai_rationale: Launch week opener. The Confessional is the highest-engagement series — leads with intrigue and establishes the anonymous confession format immediately.

content_data.confessionText: I told my mom the decorator was fully booked so she'd stop sending me mandap photos. The decorator was not booked.

content.ai_rationale: First confession — establishes tone. Relatable mom-bride tension, specific detail (mandap photos), self-deprecating humor.

content_data.confessionText: My MIL added 40 people to the guest list while I was on vacation. I found out from the caterer.

content.ai_rationale: Second confession in story sequence — escalates the chaos. MIL drama is universally relatable in desi weddings.

content_data.confessionText: I created a fake 'venue availability' email to convince my parents we couldn't do a Tuesday wedding.

content.ai_rationale: Third confession — mix up with a groom confession. Tuesday wedding reference is very specific to desi wedding culture (auspicious dates).
Close the story sequence with submission CTA. Drives UGC pipeline from day one.

content_data.ctaTagline: we have a tab for both of you

content.caption:
Episode 1: The guest list negotiation.

She wanted intimate. Mom wanted everyone she's ever made chai for.

Who won? You already know.

Tag someone whose mom did this.

#BridezillaVsMomzilla #DesiWedding #WeddingGuestList #TheMarigold #IndianWedding #WeddingPlanning
content.ai_rationale: First BvM post — the most universally relatable topic. Guest list size is THE desi wedding argument. Comment-bait with 'tag someone.'

content_data.description: From "discuss overall wedding vision" to "confirm the baraat horse." We thought of everything so you don't have to.

content.caption:
582 tasks. 13 phases. One platform that actually gets desi weddings.

The Marigold — built for the wedding your Pinterest board can't handle.

#WeddingPlanning #DesiWedding #TheMarigold #WeddingChecklist
content.ai_rationale: Product awareness play. The 582 stat is impressive and specific — anchors credibility. 'Baraat horse' signals cultural specificity.

content_data.quote: Your Pinterest board with 400 pins isn't a brief. It's a cry for help.
content_data.tagline: The Marigold turns 400 pins into one Brief.
content_data.seriesLabel: Things Your Vendor Wishes You Knew

content.caption:
Your photographer said what we were all thinking.

400 pins is not a vision — it's overwhelm wearing aesthetic clothing.

The Marigold turns that chaos into a single, clear Brief your vendors will actually thank you for.

#WeddingVendors #WeddingPhotography #DesiWedding #TheMarigold #WeddingPlanning
content.ai_rationale: Vendor quote — positions The Marigold as the solution to a real pain point. Vendors reshare this content, expanding reach.

options: Zen Queen — "beautiful and stress-free"
Type-A Goddess — "I have a Gantt chart"
Creative Visionary — "saving inspo since 2019"
Party Starter — "how's the DJ?"

content.caption:
Take the quiz. Share your result. No judgment (okay, a little judgment).

#BrideEnergy #WeddingQuiz #DesiWedding #TheMarigold
content.ai_rationale: Quiz series launch. Title slide goes as story with poll sticker overlay. Results posted as follow-up stories for sharing.

content_data.resultQuote: "I just want it to be beautiful. The details will work themselves out. They always do."
content_data.resultDescription: You need: a platform that handles the chaos while you stay calm. Moodboards over spreadsheets. Briefs that write themselves.

content.ai_rationale: Result A in story sequence. Shareable — brides repost their type.

content_data.resultQuote: "I have a colour-coded Gantt chart, a backup venue, and a spreadsheet for the spreadsheets."
content_data.resultDescription: You need: 582 tasks across 13 phases. Filters by status, priority, and due date. A checklist that finally matches your energy.

content.ai_rationale: Result B. The Type-A bride is the core product user — this result directly maps to Marigold features.

content_data.resultQuote: "My Pinterest has 12 boards and I know the difference between editorial and documentary photography."
content_data.resultDescription: You need: moodboards that flow across workspaces. A colour & tone slider. Style keyword chips. The Brief that makes your vendor cry happy tears.

content.ai_rationale: Result C. Appeals to the aesthetic-driven bride segment.

content_data.resultQuote: "Less planning, more dancing. Is the DJ confirmed? That's literally all I need to know."
content_data.resultDescription: You need: Guest Experience Lab for photo booths and sparkler exits. A sangeet playlist builder. And someone else to handle the rest (hi, that's us).

content.ai_rationale: Result D. The fun bride — least planning-oriented but highest personality share rate.

content.caption:
Episode 2: The lehenga color wars.

Dusty rose vs. traditional red. The battle that launched a thousand WhatsApp voice notes.

#BridezillaVsMomzilla #LehengaColor #DesiWedding #TheMarigold
content.ai_rationale: Second BvM episode. Lehenga color is highly emotional, culturally specific. Story format for quick engagement.

content_data.confessionText: I screenshot my fiancé's credit card statement to see how much the ring cost. I was underwhelmed.

content.caption:
New confession dropped. Swipe to feel seen.

Submit yours — link in bio. We'll never tell.

#TheConfessional #BrideConfessions #DesiWedding #TheMarigold
content.ai_rationale: Standalone confession story. Ring drama is universally engaging. Drives submissions CTA.

content_data.headline: Your décor palette flows to every workspace.

content.caption:
Pick your colors once. Watch them flow everywhere — from your mandap to your mehndi invites to your cake design board.

That's how The Marigold works. One palette. Every workspace. Zero inconsistency.

#WeddingPlanning #WeddingDecor #DesiWedding #TheMarigold #WeddingDesign
content.ai_rationale: Feature awareness post. Workspace sync is a differentiating product feature. Visual post format for feed aesthetics.

content_data.ctaTagline: we'll make both of you cry happy tears

content.caption:
Episode 3: Mandap décor.

Minimalist vs. maximalist. Orchids vs. every flower in the market.

The compromise? There is no compromise. Someone wins and someone posts passive-aggressive WhatsApp statuses.

Tag a bride who's living this.

#BridezillaVsMomzilla #MandapDecor #DesiWedding #TheMarigold #WeddingDecor
content.ai_rationale: BvM post format for feed presence. Mandap décor is visual and emotional. Maximalist vs. minimalist is a generational divide.

content_data.confessionText: I have three separate wedding Pinterest accounts. One for the wedding I want, one for the wedding my mom wants, and one for what we can actually afford.

content.caption:
Three Pinterest accounts. Zero peace of mind.

The Marigold: where all three versions of your wedding finally become one.

Confess at the link in bio.

#TheConfessional #WeddingPlanning #DesiWedding #TheMarigold
content.ai_rationale: Gold variant for visual variety. Three Pinterest accounts is extremely relatable and ties directly to the product (consolidation).

content_data.description: From "we're engaged!" to "we survived." Every phase. Every task. Every aunty's opinion — accounted for.

content.caption:
13 phases. From the first excited phone call to the last thank-you card.

The Marigold maps every single one.

#WeddingPlanning #DesiWedding #TheMarigold #WeddingChecklist
content.ai_rationale: Second stat post — the 13 phases number complements the 582 tasks stat from Week 1. 'Every aunty's opinion' adds brand voice.

content_data.quote: When the bride says 'I trust your vision' and then sends 47 reference photos at 11pm, I know we're in for a journey.
content_data.tagline: The Marigold Brief: one document, zero 11pm texts.
content_data.seriesLabel: Things Your Vendor Wishes You Knew

content.caption:
Your decorator has seen things.

"I trust your vision" followed by 47 photos is not trust — it's chaos wearing a polite mask.

The Marigold Brief consolidates your inspo into one clear document. Your vendors will send you flowers.

#WeddingVendors #WeddingDecor #DesiWedding #TheMarigold
content.ai_rationale: Vendor quote #2. Decorator perspective. 11pm texts is a universal vendor pain point. Product tie-in via Brief feature.

content_data.brideQuote: "Let's do a tasting menu. Small plates. Curated."
content_data.brideAnnotation: she watched one episode of Chef's Table
content_data.momQuote: "Paneer, dal makhani, three types of biryani, and a live chaat counter. Minimum."
content_data.momAnnotation: she has never let anyone leave hungry

content.caption:
Episode 4: The food fight.

Tasting menu vs. the full desi spread. She wants curated. Mom wants no one to leave without a food coma.

In every desi family, the food budget is where dreams go to negotiate.

#BridezillaVsMomzilla #WeddingFood #DesiWedding #TheMarigold
content.ai_rationale: Food is the #1 expense and #1 argument in desi weddings. Specific food items (biryani, chaat counter) signal cultural insider knowledge.

content_data.confessionText: My mom told the pandit our budget was ₹15 lakh. Our actual budget is ₹40 lakh. She's saving the rest 'for emergencies.' The emergency is a bigger wedding.

content.caption:
The budget confession.

Every desi family has a 'real budget' and a 'what we told the pandit' budget.

Confess at the link in bio.

#TheConfessional #WeddingBudget #DesiWedding #TheMarigold
content.ai_rationale: Budget secrecy is universal in desi weddings. Pandit reference adds cultural texture. Lavender variant for rotation.

options: Choreography Queen — "I have a 12-minute medley"
Shy Performer — "I'll stand in the back row"
Director Mode — "I'm not dancing, I'm producing"
Freestyle Diva — "just play the music and watch"

content.caption:
New quiz! What's your sangeet energy?

Vote on our story, then swipe for your result.

No wrong answers. Unless your answer is 'skip the sangeet.' That's wrong.

#SangeetEnergy #WeddingQuiz #DesiWedding #TheMarigold #Sangeet
content.ai_rationale: Second quiz — new theme keeping the same 4-result format. Sangeet is a uniquely South Asian event, strong cultural hook.

content_data.headline: The Vendor Brief writes itself from your moodboard.
content_data.annotation: photographer, decorator, caterer — one brief each

content.caption:
Stop sending vendors a Pinterest link and praying.

The Marigold takes your moodboard — colors, styles, references — and turns it into a clear, professional Brief for each vendor.

Photographer gets their brief. Decorator gets theirs. Everyone knows the vision. Nobody's guessing.

#WeddingPlanning #WeddingVendors #DesiWedding #TheMarigold
content.ai_rationale: Feature callout for the Brief Builder. Directly addresses the pain point from the vendor quote series.

content_data.momQuote: "Live band with a dhol player. Non-negotiable."
content_data.momAnnotation: the dhol is not optional, beta

content.caption:
Episode 5: DJ vs. live band.

She wants seamless transitions. Mom wants a dhol player who makes the floor shake.

The real answer? Both. You do both. You always do both.

#BridezillaVsMomzilla #WeddingMusic #DesiWedding #TheMarigold #DholPlayer
content.ai_rationale: Music is emotional. Dhol player is an iconic desi wedding element. 'You always do both' is the relatable truth.

content_data.confessionText: I told my fiancé the photographer costs ₹3 lakh. It costs ₹5 lakh. I'm funding the difference from my 'emergency savings.' The emergency is good photos.

content.caption:
The 'emergency savings' are for the emergency of having bad wedding photos.

Valid priority tbh.

#TheConfessional #WeddingPhotography #DesiWedding #TheMarigold
content.ai_rationale: Photography budget lies — very specific, very relatable. Mirrors the budget theme from Week 4 confession but different angle.

content_data.quote: The couple who trusts the timeline is the couple who actually enjoys their wedding. The couple who 'wings it' is the couple crying in the parking lot at 6pm.
content_data.tagline: The Marigold timeline: hour by hour, event by event.
content_data.seriesLabel: Things Your Vendor Wishes You Knew

content.caption:
A wedding coordinator who has seen things.

Timelines aren't Type-A — they're the difference between enjoying your baraat and having a breakdown in the parking lot.

The Marigold builds your timeline hour by hour, across every event, every ceremony, every function.

#WeddingVendors #WeddingTimeline #DesiWedding #TheMarigold
content.ai_rationale: Vendor quote #3. Timeline/coordination angle. 'Crying in the parking lot' is vivid and real. Product tie-in via timeline feature.

content_data.description: Mehndi. Haldi. Sangeet. Ceremony. Reception. And that's before the after-party. Each one gets its own workspace, its own checklist, its own vibe.

content.caption:
One wedding, seven events.

Each with its own décor, its own timeline, its own vendor coordination, its own guest list drama.

The Marigold gives each event its own workspace so nothing bleeds into everything else.

#DesiWedding #WeddingPlanning #TheMarigold #IndianWedding
content.ai_rationale: The multi-event stat differentiates desi weddings from Western ones. 7 events is a strong number. Workspace-per-event is a key product feature.

content_data.momQuote: "The Gupta's did it at Grand Hyatt. We're doing Grand Hyatt."

content.caption:
Episode 6: The venue wars.

She wants a garden. Mom wants the same hotel as Mrs. Gupta's son's wedding.

Nobody's bringing up the monsoon. Nobody.

#BridezillaVsMomzilla #WeddingVenue #DesiWedding #TheMarigold
content.ai_rationale: Venue is the biggest decision. The Gupta comparison adds social pressure humor. Monsoon oversight is a real planning fail.

content_data.confessionText: I follow 14 wedding planning accounts and I haven't started planning my own wedding. It's in four months.

content.caption:
Following wedding accounts is not the same as planning a wedding. We had to say it.

Four months out and the only thing planned is the Pinterest board.

We see you. Start here.

#TheConfessional #WeddingPlanning #DesiWedding #TheMarigold
content.ai_rationale: Procrastination confession — calls out the behavior gently. Direct product hook: 'Start here' positions Marigold as the first step.

content.caption:
Got a confession? Drop it anonymously — link in bio.

We've heard everything. Literally everything.

#TheConfessional #DesiWedding #TheMarigold
content.ai_rationale: Mid-cycle CTA to drive more submissions. Keeps the UGC pipeline active.

content_data.headline: One checklist. Every ceremony. Nothing forgotten.
content_data.annotation: mehndi tasks, haldi tasks, sangeet tasks — all organized

content.caption:
The mehndi needs its own checklist. So does the haldi. So does the sangeet. So does every other event you're planning simultaneously while also working a full-time job.

The Marigold separates it all. One master checklist, broken down by event, by phase, by priority.

Nothing falls through the cracks.

#WeddingPlanning #WeddingChecklist #DesiWedding #TheMarigold
content.ai_rationale: Feature callout for the multi-event checklist. 'Also working a full-time job' adds empathy.

content_data.brideQuote: "I want to walk in with my dad. Just us."
content_data.brideAnnotation: she's been dreaming of this moment
content_data.momQuote: "The whole family walks in together. That's how it's done."
content_data.ctaTagline: we'll make sure nobody trips on the aisle

content.caption:
Episode 7: The entrance.

One of you wants a cinematic dad-daughter moment. The other wants a full family procession with the cousins.

Both are beautiful. Both will make everyone cry. Plan accordingly.

#BridezillaVsMomzilla #WeddingEntrance #DesiWedding #TheMarigold
content.ai_rationale: Emotional topic. The entrance is deeply personal. Modern vs. traditional tension. Lower conflict than guest list — adds warmth to the series.

content_data.confessionText: My cousin offered to do my makeup 'for free.' I said yes to be polite. I've been quietly booking a professional and haven't told anyone.

content.caption:
The polite yes followed by the secret professional booking. A desi wedding tradition as old as time.

Confess something worse — link in bio.

#TheConfessional #WeddingMakeup #DesiWedding #TheMarigold
content.ai_rationale: Family-offering-services-you-don't-want is deeply desi. Makeup is a safe, relatable topic. Humorous without being mean.

content_data.quote: I've been asked to make a 7-tier cake for 400 guests with a ₹50,000 budget. The audacity was delicious.
content_data.tagline: The Marigold helps you budget before you ask.
content_data.seriesLabel: Things Your Vendor Wishes You Knew

content.caption:
The baker has spoken.

7 tiers. 400 guests. Budget: vibes and a prayer.

Before you send that inquiry, The Marigold helps you build a realistic budget by vendor category so nobody has to have that conversation.

#WeddingVendors #WeddingCake #DesiWedding #TheMarigold #WeddingBudget
content.ai_rationale: Vendor quote #4. Baker perspective is fun and light. Budget mismatch humor. Product tie-in via budget feature.

content_data.brideAnnotation: she's been saving the Pinterest board since 2020
content_data.momQuote: "Why not Tirupati first? Blessings, then beach."
content_data.momAnnotation: she's already called the travel agent

content.caption:
Episode 8: Honeymoon planning.

She wants Amalfi. Mom wants Tirupati first, then Amalfi, then stop at the family house in Hyderabad.

It's not a honeymoon, it's a pilgrimage with a detour.

#BridezillaVsMomzilla #Honeymoon #DesiWedding #TheMarigold
content.ai_rationale: Honeymoon interference is a lighter, fun episode. Tirupati-first is a very specific South Indian reference. Broadens cultural specificity.

content_data.confessionText: I agreed to a morning ceremony, an afternoon lunch, an evening reception, AND a late-night after-party. That's four events in one day. I haven't slept since the engagement.

content.caption:
Four events. One day. Zero sleep.

This is not a wedding, it's an endurance sport.

The Marigold helps you plan each event separately so your brain doesn't melt.

#TheConfessional #WeddingDay #DesiWedding #TheMarigold
content.ai_rationale: The multi-event-single-day grind. Extremely relatable for desi weddings. Product tie-in to per-event workspaces.

options: The Over-Explainer — sends voice notes longer than the ceremony
The Ghost — books them and disappears until the week before
The Micro-Manager — wants daily progress photos
The Vibes-Only — "you'll know it when you see it"

content.caption:
New quiz! How do you communicate with your vendors?

Be honest. Your decorator already knows.

#VendorVibes #WeddingQuiz #DesiWedding #TheMarigold
content.ai_rationale: Third quiz theme — vendor communication. Ties into the vendor quote series. All 4 types are recognizable and funny.

content_data.description: Photographer. Videographer. Decorator. Caterer. Mehndi artist. Dhol player. Pandit. And 40 more you haven't thought of yet.

content.caption:
You think you've got all your vendors covered? There are 47 categories.

The baraat horse coordinator is a real vendor. The ice sculptor is a real vendor. The parking valet for 400 cars is a real vendor.

The Marigold tracks all of them.

#WeddingVendors #DesiWedding #TheMarigold #WeddingPlanning
content.ai_rationale: Vendor count stat — large number creates surprise. 'Baraat horse coordinator' is funny and real. Shows product depth.

content_data.brideQuote: "Candid only. No posed shots. Editorial vibes."
content_data.brideAnnotation: she has a shot list anyway
content_data.momQuote: "I need a family photo with every uncle. Every. Single. One."
content_data.ctaTagline: we scheduled 2 hours for uncle photos

content.caption:
Episode 9: Photography style.

She wants candid editorial. Mom needs a posed portrait with every relative who's ever attended a family function.

The compromise: candid for the couple, posed for the uncles, three extra hours for the photographer.

#BridezillaVsMomzilla #WeddingPhotography #DesiWedding #TheMarigold
content.ai_rationale: Photography debate is universal but the 'uncle photos' angle is very desi. 94 uncles is absurd and funny.

content_data.confessionText: My future MIL asked to approve my bridal jewelry. I said 'of course, aunty!' and then bought exactly what I wanted anyway.

content.caption:
"Of course, aunty!" — the most powerful phrase in desi wedding diplomacy.

Said with a smile. Executed with precision.

#TheConfessional #BridalJewelry #DesiWedding #TheMarigold
content.ai_rationale: MIL jewelry approval is deeply relatable. The 'of course aunty' with secret defiance captures the bride's reality.

content_data.quote: I got a brief that said 'modern but traditional, bold but subtle, colorful but muted.' That's not a brief, that's a riddle.
content_data.tagline: The Marigold Brief: clear direction, zero contradictions.
content_data.seriesLabel: Things Your Vendor Wishes You Knew

content.caption:
"Modern but traditional." "Bold but subtle."

Your decorator isn't a mind reader — they're a professional who needs a clear direction.

The Marigold Brief eliminates the contradictions. Your style keywords, your color palette, your references — organized into one clear document per vendor.

#WeddingVendors #WeddingDecor #DesiWedding #TheMarigold
content.ai_rationale: Vendor quote #5. The contradictory brief is hilarious and real. Perfect product tie-in — the Brief feature solves exactly this.

content_data.momQuote: "Baraat at 3. Ceremony at 6. Dinner at 9. Dancing until the sun comes up."
content_data.momAnnotation: sleep is for after the wedding

content.caption:
Episode 10: The timeline.

Done by 11 vs. dancing until sunrise. One of you is realistic. The other is your mom.

Neither of you will get what you want. The wedding ends when the dhol player's arms give out.

#BridezillaVsMomzilla #WeddingTimeline #DesiWedding #TheMarigold
content.ai_rationale: Timeline is practical but the conflict is real. 'Dhol player's arms give out' is a great visual punchline.

content_data.confessionText: I hired a day-of coordinator specifically to manage my mother-in-law. The MIL doesn't know. The coordinator does.

content.caption:
A coordinator whose real job is MIL management.

The most strategic hire in the history of desi weddings.

#TheConfessional #WeddingCoordinator #DesiWedding #TheMarigold
content.ai_rationale: MIL management coordinator — specific, strategic, and savvy. Shows brides being smart about their planning.

content_data.headline: Give mom her own login. Let her see her tasks.
content_data.annotation: her task: stay in her lane (lovingly)

content.caption:
Give your mom access to the platform. She sees her tasks — approving the guest list, coordinating with her side of the family, choosing return gift options.

She feels included. You stay in control. Everyone wins. Nobody sends passive-aggressive WhatsApp statuses.

#WeddingPlanning #DesiWedding #TheMarigold #FamilyPlanning
content.ai_rationale: Family access feature — directly addresses the mom-bride dynamic from BvM series. 'Stay in her lane (lovingly)' is on-brand humor.

options: The Spreadsheet — every rupee tracked, every vendor negotiated
The Vibes Budget — "it'll work out somehow"
The Secret Stash — hidden savings account for the things that matter
The Family Fund — "papa said don't worry about money"

content.caption:
What's your budget energy? Be honest — no one's checking your bank statements.

Swipe for your result.

#BudgetEnergy #WeddingQuiz #DesiWedding #TheMarigold #WeddingBudget
content.ai_rationale: Fourth quiz theme — budget energy. Ties into confession themes about budget secrets. 'Papa said don't worry' is very desi.

content_data.brideAnnotation: she's been practicing in the mirror
content_data.momQuote: "12-minute family medley. Matching outfits. Fog machine."
content_data.momAnnotation: she's been watching sangeet videos on YouTube
content_data.ctaTagline: we scheduled rehearsal time for both of you

content.caption:
Episode 11: Sangeet choreography.

3-minute clean number vs. 12-minute Bollywood production with costume changes.

Mom has already downloaded the songs. Dad has already said he's not dancing. He will dance.

#BridezillaVsMomzilla #Sangeet #DesiWedding #TheMarigold #SangeetDance
content.ai_rationale: Sangeet is THE desi wedding content moment. Fog machine is absurd and real. Dad refusing but eventually dancing is universal.

content_data.confessionText: We wanted a destination wedding in Udaipur. My dad said 'sure, for 50 guests.' We now have 600 guests in Udaipur. My dad hasn't spoken about the budget since.

content.caption:
600 guests. In Udaipur. The budget? We don't talk about the budget.

Destination weddings: the idea is small and intimate. The execution is your entire extended family at a palace.

#TheConfessional #DestinationWedding #DesiWedding #TheMarigold
content.ai_rationale: Destination wedding scope creep. 50 → 600 is the perfect escalation. Dad's silence is funny and real.

content_data.description: Mehndi, haldi, sangeet, ceremony, reception — and that's before the post-wedding brunch, the puja, and the 'casual family dinner' for 200.

content.caption:
4.5 events average. Some of you are doing 7.

Each event needs its own timeline, its own vendor list, its own guest count, its own vibe.

The Marigold treats each one as its own workspace. Because they are.

#DesiWedding #WeddingPlanning #TheMarigold #IndianWedding
content.ai_rationale: Event count stat — reinforces the multi-event complexity message. 'Casual family dinner for 200' is pitch-perfect voice.

content_data.brideAnnotation: she's been on Etsy for weeks
content_data.momQuote: "Dry fruit boxes. Big ones. With our family name printed on top."
content_data.momAnnotation: she already has a dry fruit guy

content.caption:
Season finale: Return gifts.

Custom candles vs. industrial-grade dry fruit boxes with the family name embossed in gold.

You know who won. The dry fruit guy always wins.

#BridezillaVsMomzilla #ReturnGifts #DesiWedding #TheMarigold
content.ai_rationale: Season 1 finale — return gifts is lighter fare to end on. 'She already has a dry fruit guy' is the perfect punchline. Dry fruit boxes are THE desi wedding favor.

content_data.confessionText: I started a wedding planning spreadsheet in Google Sheets. It now has 23 tabs, 4 pivot tables, and I still don't know if the caterer is confirmed.

content.caption:
23 tabs. 4 pivot tables. Zero clarity.

This is exactly why we built The Marigold. Your spreadsheet era is over.

#TheConfessional #WeddingPlanning #DesiWedding #TheMarigold #SpreadsheetChaos
content.ai_rationale: The spreadsheet confession is the ultimate product-market-fit moment. Directly positions Marigold as the upgrade from Google Sheets.

content_data.quote: The best clients aren't the ones with the biggest budgets. They're the ones who know what they want before the first meeting.
content_data.tagline: The Marigold helps you know what you want — before you ask for it.
content_data.seriesLabel: Things Your Vendor Wishes You Knew

content.caption:
The best thing you can bring to a vendor meeting isn't money — it's clarity.

Know your style. Know your budget range. Know your non-negotiables.

The Marigold helps you figure all of this out before you send a single inquiry.

#WeddingVendors #WeddingPlanning #DesiWedding #TheMarigold
content.ai_rationale: Vendor quote #6. Positive framing — what makes a great client. Positions Marigold as the tool that makes brides better clients.

content_data.headline: Guest list. RSVPs. Seating chart. All connected.
content_data.annotation: aunty next to aunty she hasn't spoken to since 2019? not on our watch

content.caption:
The guest list is not a spreadsheet. It's a political document.

Who sits where. Who can't sit near whom. Which aunties are in a cold war. Which cousins just broke up.

The Marigold maps it all — guest list, RSVPs, dietary needs, seating assignments, family dynamics. Connected.

#WeddingPlanning #GuestList #DesiWedding #TheMarigold #SeatingChart
content.ai_rationale: Feature teaser for guest management. 'Aunty cold war' is perfect voice. Seating chart as political document resonates deeply.

content_data.confessionText: I told everyone the wedding is 'intimate' because it sounds intentional. The truth is we ran out of budget after the sangeet catering bill.

content.caption:
"Intimate" is just "we ran out of budget" in a prettier font.

Respect to every bride who made budget look like an aesthetic choice.

#TheConfessional #IntimateWedding #DesiWedding #TheMarigold
content.ai_rationale: Budget-as-aesthetic confession. Reframes a common situation with humor. End of Month 3 — keeps the series fresh.

options: The Diplomat — "great idea, aunty!" (does own thing anyway)
The Includer — gives her real tasks so she feels needed
The Boundaries Queen — "this is our decision, but thank you"
The Tag Team — sends fiancé to handle all mom communications

content.caption:
The quiz your MIL should never see.

content_data.description: One place for the guest list, the budget, the vendors, the timeline, the moodboard, the checklist, the seating chart, and the sanity. Especially the sanity.

Just The Marigold.
